bibliographic_data.title: Comprehending Semantic Types in JSON Data with Graph Neural Networks
bibliographic_data.abstract: Semantic types are a more powerful and detailed way of describing data than atomic types such as strings or integers. They establish connections between columns and concepts from the real world, providing more nuanced and fine-grained information that can be useful for tasks such as automated data cleaning, schema matching, and data discovery. Existing deep learning models trained on large text corpora have been successful at performing single-column semantic type prediction for relational data. However, in this work, we propose an extension of the semantic type prediction problem to JSON data, labeling the types based on JSON Paths. Our model outperforms a state-of-the-art existing model in several cases. These results demonstrate the ability of our model to understand complex JSON data and its potential usage for JSON-related data processing tasks.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper proposes a novel semantic type classification model for semi-structured JSON data, which outperforms existing models on certain semantic types.
core_content.key_methodology: A graph neural network is used to comprehend the structural information within collections of JSON documents. The model uses the same set of features as Sherlock, a single-column prediction model, but extends it to JSON data by labeling types based on JSON Paths.
outcomes: The proposed model outperforms a state-of-the-art existing model in several cases.
Demonstrates the ability to understand complex JSON data and its potential usage for JSON-related data processing tasks.
core_content.full_text_markdown: Detecting semantic types of columns in relational tables can be useful for various data preparation and information extraction tasks such as data cleaning and integration. Traditional systems classify table columns into atomic types like Boolean, integer, or string. Semantic types provide a finer-grained classification that connects to real-world concepts. The paper proposes a novel model using graph neural networks to predict semantic types in JSON data based on JSON Paths. This approach extends the existing Sherlock model, which is trained on relational data, to handle semi-structured JSON documents. The outcomes show improved accuracy and F1 scores for certain semantic types.
